summary.intent: Fix a flaky E2E test in CI that intermittently failed to find 'Matrix (moltis-testbot)' text in the channels settings page.
summary.outcome: Identified a race condition where Preact signal updates weren't flushed to the DOM before page.evaluate() returned. Fixed by adding a requestAnimationFrame wait after prefetchChannels(), matching the pattern already used in the shared test helper. Test passed 5/5 runs after the fix.

repo: Preact signal updates (channels.value) are batched and rendered asynchronously — DOM is not updated synchronously after signal assignment.
The shared test helper at settings-nav.spec.js:85 already uses 'await new Promise((resolve) => requestAnimationFrame(() => resolve()))' as the canonical pattern for flushing Preact renders in tests.
channels is a module-level signal (not component state), so it persists across re-renders and component lifecycle.
The mock WebSocket setup uses state.pending[req.id] to resolve RPCs synchronously — sendRpc stores resolvers in S.pending before calling ws.send().
setConnected(true) inside page.evaluate() triggers a useEffect in ChannelsPage that calls loadChannels(), creating a second RPC in addition to the explicit prefetchChannels() call.

code.finding: Canonical rAF flush pattern: 'await new Promise((resolve) => requestAnimationFrame(() => resolve()))' — used in shared helper to wait for Preact render commit.
Bug location: missing rAF wait after prefetchChannels() in the pending matrix sender test. Fixed by adding the same rAF pattern from the helper.
sendRpc checks ws.readyState !== WebSocket.OPEN before sending; mock must set readyState to WebSocket.OPEN (or 1) to pass this guard.

workflow: Previous stabilization attempts were already made on this test (visible in git log) — repeat flakiness suggests the rAF pattern was missed on the second prefetchChannels() call site.
Running a flaky test 5 times in a loop is a practical way to confirm stability after a race condition fix.
Playwright's toBeVisible() 10s retry timeout is insufficient to recover from a race where Preact never gets a chance to flush due to early evaluate() return on slower CI runners.

friction: CI logs were mostly download/build output, requiring grep to find actual test failure messages.
The root cause required deep analysis of Preact's async rendering model, signal batching, and the interaction between useEffect dependencies and signal subscriptions.
Multiple red herrings investigated: module deduplication, mock WS readyState, second loadChannels() RPC from the useEffect triggered by setConnected.
open_items: Other tests using setConnected(true) without prefetchChannels() (around lines 678 and 762) were checked but not deeply audited — they may have subtler timing issues.

The useEffect in ChannelsPage fires loadChannels() when connected changes, creating a second RPC alongside explicit prefetchChannels() calls — this double-fetch is architectural noise worth reviewing.
